examples.title: Algorithm: Data Classification
examples.description: Classify data into numerical or categorical types
examples.code:
ALGORITHM classifyData(data)
INPUT: data (a dataset)
OUTPUT: classification (numerical or categorical)

BEGIN
 IF data is a number THEN
 classification = 'numerical'
 ELSE IF data is a category THEN
 classification = 'categorical'
 END IF
 RETURN classification
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates a simple data classification algorithm. It checks if the input data is a number or a category and returns the corresponding classification.

examples.title: Python: Data Classification
examples.description: Classify data into numerical or categorical types using Python
examples.explanation: This Python code defines a function `classify_data` that takes in a dataset and returns its classification as 'numerical' or 'categorical'. It uses the `isinstance` function to check the type of the input data.

examples.title: C: Data Classification
examples.description: Classify data into numerical or categorical types using C
examples.explanation: This C code defines a function `classify_data` that takes in a void pointer to a dataset and returns its classification as 'numerical' or 'categorical'. It uses pointer casting to check the type of the input data.
